global.title: Document editors follow a component-based React architecture with Tailwind CSS styling
global.content:
Document editors in Powerhouse are React components following a component-based architecture:

- `editor.tsx` - Main editor wrapper (entry point)
- Container component (e.g., `TodoList.tsx`) - Orchestrates child components, reads state via hook
- List component (e.g., `Todos.tsx`) - Renders collections
- Item component (e.g., `Todo.tsx`) - Individual items with edit/delete
- Form component (e.g., `AddTodo.tsx`) - Handles new item creation

Styling options:
1. **Default HTML styling** - Standard tags render with boilerplate styles
2. **Tailwind CSS** - Built into Connect, use utility classes directly
3. **Custom CSS files** - Import traditional .css files

Pattern details:
- Use `useSelectedTodoListDocument()` hook for state + dispatch in any component
- Import action creators from generated code
- Use React local state (`useState`) for UI-only state (e.g., `isEditing` toggle)
- Use document dispatch for persistent data changes
- Built-in actions like `setName` from `document-model/document` for document metadata
- `DocumentToolbar` component from design system provides standard document actions
global.description: Editors decompose into container, list, item, and form components. Styling options: default HTML, Tailwind CSS, or custom CSS.
